cves.description: A SQL injection vulnerability in the login form allows attackers to bypass authentication by injecting malicious SQL statements.
cves.cvss3_rationale: Score of 9.8: attack is network-accessible; low attack complexity; no privileges required; no user interaction needed; high confidentiality impact; high integrity impact; high availability impact.
cves.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
cves.cwe_description: The product constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input.
cves.explanation: This CVE is classified as CWE-89 (SQL Injection) because the description references SQL injection and authentication bypass.

common_confusions.name: Cross-site Scripting
common_confusions.differentiator: XSS targets browser-rendered HTML, not database queries

common_confusions.name: OS Command Injection
common_confusions.differentiator: OS Command Injection targets system commands, not SQL

cves.description: An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the image processing library allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted PNG file.
cves.cvss3_rationale: Score of 7.8: attack requires local access; low attack complexity; no privileges required; requires user interaction; high confidentiality impact; high integrity impact; high availability impact.
cves.cvss4_rationale: Score of 8.2: attack requires local access; low attack complexity; no privileges required; active user interaction required.
cves.cwe_description: The product writes data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer.
cves.explanation: This CVE is classified as CWE-787 because the description mentions out-of-bounds write and memory corruption.

common_confusions.name: Out-of-bounds Read
common_confusions.differentiator: Out-of-bounds read leaks data; out-of-bounds write corrupts data

common_confusions.name: Buffer Errors
common_confusions.differentiator: CWE-119 is the parent category covering all buffer operations

cves.description: Improper authentication in the admin panel allows unauthenticated remote attackers to access sensitive configuration settings.
cves.cvss3_rationale: Score of 7.5: attack is network-accessible; low attack complexity; no privileges required; no user interaction needed; high confidentiality impact.
cves.cwe_description: When an actor claims to have a given identity, the product does not prove or insufficiently proves that the claim is correct.
cves.explanation: This CVE is classified as CWE-287 because the description mentions improper authentication and unauthorized access.

common_confusions.name: Missing Authentication
common_confusions.differentiator: CWE-306 is missing authentication entirely; CWE-287 is improper authentication

cves.description: Use of a broken cryptographic algorithm in the session token generation allows attackers to predict session tokens and hijack user sessions.
cves.cvss3_rationale: Score of 5.9: attack is network-accessible; high attack complexity; no privileges required; high confidentiality impact.
cves.cwe_name: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
cves.cwe_description: The product uses a broken or risky cryptographic algorithm or protocol.
cves.explanation: This CVE is classified as CWE-327 because the description mentions broken cryptographic algorithm usage.

common_confusions.name: Insufficient Randomness
common_confusions.differentiator: CWE-330 is about weak randomness; CWE-327 is about weak cryptographic algorithms

cves.description: A race condition in the file locking mechanism allows concurrent processes to corrupt shared data structures leading to denial of service.
cves.cvss3_rationale: Score of 5.1: attack requires local access; high attack complexity; no privileges required; high availability impact.
cves.cwe_name: Concurrent Execution Using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')
cves.cwe_description: The product contains a code sequence that can run concurrently with other code.
cves.explanation: This CVE is classified as CWE-362 because the description references race condition and concurrent access.

common_confusions.name: TOCTOU Race Condition
common_confusions.differentiator: TOCTOU is a specific type of race condition between check and use
